---
title: "Thesis Codes"
author: "Deborah"
date: "2025-07-29"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---


```{r}
# Core wrangling & visualization
library(tidyverse)     # data manipulation
library(ggplot2)       # visualization
library(patchwork)     # combining multiple plots
library(tibble)        # tidy data frames
library(scales)        # axis scaling & formatting (e.g., percent, commas)
library(knitr)         # for kable() tables


# Modeling & evaluation
library(grid)          # base R graphics system (for layout, spacing, unit functions)
library(caret)         # modeling, training, cross-validation, confusionMatrix
library(nnet)          # multinomial logistic regression
library(ranger)        # random forest implementation
library(xgboost)       # gradient boosting (XGBoost)
library(keras)         # deep learning (LSTM)
library(tabnet)        # TabNet deep learning model for tabular data
library(torch)         # backend for tabnet & deep learning
library(corrplot)      # correlation heatmap visualization

# Shiny app (Decision Support System)
library(shiny)         # Shiny framework
library(shinyWidgets)  # enhanced Shiny UI widgets
library(bslib)         # Shiny Bootstrap themes
library(DT)            # interactive tables for Shiny


```

1. Data Loading
```{r}
# Load dataset into R environment
getwd()
setwd("C:/Users/welcome/Desktop")
diabetes_data <- read.csv("diabetes_012_health_indicators_BRFSS2021.csv")

# Preview first few rows of the dataset
head(diabetes_data)

```

2. Data Quality Checks
```{r}
# Display the structure of the dataset
str(diabetes_data)
```


```{r}
# Display summary statistics (min, max, median, mean, quartiles) for each variable
summary(diabetes_data)

```


```{r}
# Check for missing values across all variables
colSums(is.na(diabetes_data))
```


```{r}
# Count the number of duplicated rows
sum(duplicated(diabetes_data))
```


```{r}
# Identify rows that appear more than once
duplicates_summary <- diabetes_data %>%
  group_by_all() %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  filter(count > 1)

head(duplicates_summary)
View(duplicates_summary)
dup_sample <- diabetes_data[duplicated(diabetes_data) | duplicated(diabetes_data, fromLast = TRUE), ]
head(dup_sample[, c("BMI", "Age", "HighBP", "HighChol", "GenHlth", "PhysHlth", "Income", "Diabetes_012")], 10)

```


```{r}
# Move the target variable (Diabetes_012) to the last column for convenience
diabetes_data <- relocate(diabetes_data, Diabetes_012, .after = last_col())
head(diabetes_data)
```

3. Exploratory Data Analysis
```{r}
# Visualize class distribution of the target variable (check for imbalance)

ggplot(diabetes_data, aes(x = factor(Diabetes_012, labels = c("No","Pre","Yes")))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(
    stat = "count",
    aes(label = comma(after_stat(count))),
    vjust = -0.3, size = 3
  ) +
  labs(x = "Diabetes Status", y = "Count") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +  # a little headroom for labels
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
# Numeric summaries of selected variables grouped by diabetes status

num_cols <- c("BMI","Age","GenHlth","MentHlth","PhysHlth","Income")

desc_table <- diabetes_data %>%
  select(all_of(num_cols), Diabetes_012) %>%
  group_by(Diabetes_012) %>%
  summarize(across(
    all_of(num_cols),
    list(
      mean  = ~ round(mean(.),1),
      median= ~ median(.),
      min   = ~ min(.),
      max   = ~ max(.)
    ), .names = "{col}_{fn}"
  )) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Diabetes_012, names_to = c("Variable","Metric"), names_sep = "_") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Metric, values_from = value)

print(desc_table)

```


```{r}
# Histogram + density overlay plots for continuous variables

hist_plots <- lapply(num_cols, function(var) {
  ggplot(diabetes_data, aes_string(x = var)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = .6) +
    geom_density(aes(y = ..count..), color = "red", size = .5) +
    ggtitle(var) +
    theme_minimal()
})
wrap_plots(hist_plots, ncol = 3)
```


```{r}
# Boxplots of continuous variables stratified by diabetes status

continuous_cols <- c("BMI", "MentHlth", "PhysHlth")

# Build boxplots for those
box_plots <- lapply(continuous_cols, function(var) {
  ggplot(diabetes_data,
         aes_string(x = "factor(Diabetes_012)",
                    y = var,
                    fill = "factor(Diabetes_012)")) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = .7) +
    labs(x = "Diabetes Status", y = var) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
})

# 3. Combine into a grid (3 panels in one row)
wrap_plots(box_plots, ncol = 3)
```


```{r}
# Proportional stacked bar charts for clinical risk factors by diabetes status

clinical_vars <- c("HighBP", "HighChol", "Stroke", "HeartDiseaseorAttack")

diabetes_data %>%
  select(all_of(clinical_vars), Diabetes_012) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(clinical_vars), 
               names_to = "feature", 
               values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(value), fill = factor(Diabetes_012))) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill", width = 0.6) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ feature,
      ncol = 2,
      scales = "free_x",
      strip.position = "bottom"   # only here
    ) +
    labs(
      x    = "0 = No, 1 = Yes",
      y    = "% within Diabetes Status",
      fill = "Diabetes Status"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      strip.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position  = "bottom"
    )
```


```{r}
# Proportional stacked bar charts for lifestyle behaviours by diabetes status

lifestyle_vars <- c("Smoker", "PhysActivity", "Fruits", "Veggies", "HvyAlcoholConsump")

diabetes_data %>%
  select(all_of(lifestyle_vars), Diabetes_012) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(lifestyle_vars), names_to = "feature", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(value), fill = factor(Diabetes_012))) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill", width = 0.6) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ feature,
      ncol = 3,
      scales = "free_x",
      strip.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      x    = "0 = No, 1 = Yes",
      y    = "% within Diabetes Status",
      fill = "Diabetes Status"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      strip.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position  = "bottom"
    )

```


```{r}
# Proportional stacked bar charts for healthcare access & functional status variables

access_vars <- c("AnyHealthcare", "NoDocbcCost", "DiffWalk", "Sex")

diabetes_data %>%
  select(all_of(access_vars), Diabetes_012) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(access_vars), names_to = "feature", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(value), fill = factor(Diabetes_012))) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill", width = 0.6) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ feature,
      ncol = 2,                # two columns → two rows
      scales = "free_x",
      strip.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      x    = "0 = No, 1 = Yes",
      y    = "% within Diabetes Status",
      fill = "Diabetes Status"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      strip.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      panel.spacing    = unit(1.5, "lines")
    )
```


4. Feature Engineering / Screening
```{r}
# Correlation matrix of selected continuous variables
corr_mat <- cor(diabetes_data %>% select(all_of(num_cols)))
corrplot(corr_mat, method = "color", tl.cex = 0.8)
```


```{r}
# Spearman correlation between predictors and target variable

# Define your predictors
feat_cols <- c("HighBP","HighChol","Smoker","Stroke","HeartDiseaseorAttack",
               "PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies","HvyAlcoholConsump",
               "AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost","DiffWalk","Sex",
               "BMI","GenHlth","MentHlth","PhysHlth","Age","Education","Income")

# Compute, tidy, sort, and plot in one go
sapply(diabetes_data[feat_cols], 
       function(x) cor(x, diabetes_data$Diabetes_012, method="spearman")) %>%
  enframe("Feature","Corr") %>%
  arrange(Corr) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Corr, reorder(Feature, Corr))) +
    geom_col(fill="steelblue") +
    labs(x="Spearman ρ", y=NULL, title="Feature–Target Spearman Correlations") +
    theme_minimal(base_size=14)


```


```{r}
# Compute NZV metrics

nzv_metrics <- nearZeroVar(diabetes_data, saveMetrics = TRUE) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Feature")

# Scatter freqRatio vs percentUnique and highlight nzv
ggplot(nzv_metrics, aes(x = freqRatio, y = percentUnique, label = Feature)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = nzv), size = 3) +
  geom_text(data = subset(nzv_metrics, nzv), 
            vjust = -1, size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black","red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Near‑Zero‑Variance Metrics",
    subtitle = "Features flagged in red have near‑zero variance",
    x = "Frequency Ratio",
    y = "% Unique Values",
    color = "NZV Flag"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

```


```{r}
# Identify near-zero variance (NZV) predictors

nzv_metrics <- nearZeroVar(diabetes_data, saveMetrics = TRUE) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Feature")

# Which ones are flagged?
nzv_metrics %>% 
  filter(nzv) %>% 
  select(Feature, freqRatio, percentUnique)

# In our case:
drop_nzv <- c("CholCheck", "AnyHealthcare", "Stroke")

# Pruned dataset
df_nzv <- diabetes_data %>% select(-all_of(drop_nzv))

```


```{r}
# Drop identified NZV variables from dataset

drop_features <- c("CholCheck", "AnyHealthcare")

# Create a pruned dataset
diabetes_data_pruned <- diabetes_data %>%
  select(-all_of(drop_features))

# Confirm removal
setdiff(drop_features, names(diabetes_data_pruned))
#> character(0)  # means all were successfully dropped

# Check remaining columns
glimpse(diabetes_data_pruned)


```

5. Train-Test Split
```{r}
#Create Stratified split, to split by class to handle class imbalance

set.seed(123)

# Stratified 80/20 split to preserve class proportions
train_idx <- createDataPartition(
  diabetes_data_pruned$Diabetes_012,
  p    = 0.8,
  list = FALSE
)

# Subset into training and testing sets
train <- diabetes_data_pruned[train_idx, ]
test  <- diabetes_data_pruned[-train_idx, ]

# ── save test labels for later evaluation ───
split_test_labels <- test$Diabetes_012

# Compare class distribution between training and test sets
prop_train <- prop.table(table(train$Diabetes_012))
prop_test  <- prop.table(table(test$Diabetes_012))

# Print the proportions side by side
data.frame(
  Class       = names(prop_train),
  Train_Pct   = round(100 * as.numeric(prop_train), 1),
  Test_Pct    = round(100 * as.numeric(prop_test), 1)
)

```


6. Handling Class Imbalance
```{r}
# handling class imbalance with the caret package by duplicating the two minority classes up to the majority class size

set.seed(123)

# Apply up-sampling so minority classes match the majority class size
train_x <- train %>% select(-Diabetes_012)
train_y <- factor(train$Diabetes_012, levels = c(0,1,2), labels = c("No","Pre","Yes"))

upsampled <- upSample(x = train_x, y = train_y, yname = "Diabetes_012")

# Balanced training dataset
balanced_training_set <- upsampled


# Quick class‐balance check
round(prop.table(table(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012)) * 100, 1)

```


```{r}
# ---- Class distribution before and after resampling ----

# Example counts (replace with your actual counts if needed)
before_counts <- data.frame(
  Class = c("No diabetes", "Prediabetes", "Diabetes"),
  Percentage = c(83.4, 2.4, 14.2),
  Dataset = "Before Resampling"
)

after_counts <- data.frame(
  Class = c("No diabetes", "Prediabetes", "Diabetes"),
  Percentage = c(33.3, 33.3, 33.3),
  Dataset = "After Resampling"
)

# Combine into one dataset
class_dist <- rbind(before_counts, after_counts)

# Plot
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(class_dist, aes(x = Class, y = Percentage, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Percentage, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e")) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Outcome Class Distribution",
    subtitle = "Before vs After Resampling",
    x = "Diabetes Status",
    y = "Percentage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

```


7. Model Training (Baseline Machine Learning Models)
```{r cache=TRUE}
# Train multinomial logistic regression
set.seed(123)
lr_model <- nnet::multinom(Diabetes_012 ~ ., data = balanced_training_set, trace = FALSE)

# Predict on test
lr_pred <- predict(lr_model, newdata = test)

# Ensure test$Diabetes_012 is a factor with matching levels
test$Diabetes_012 <- factor(
  test$Diabetes_012,
  levels = c(0, 1, 2),
  labels = c("No", "Pre", "Yes")
)


# Evaluate
lr_cm <- confusionMatrix(lr_pred, test$Diabetes_012)

# Print with custom heading
cat("Logistic Regression:\n\n")
print(lr_cm)


```


```{r cache=TRUE}
# Train Random Forest model using ranger
set.seed(123)
rf_model <- ranger(
  formula        = Diabetes_012 ~ .,
  data           = balanced_training_set,
  num.trees      = 500,
  classification = TRUE,
  verbose        = TRUE
)

# Predict on the test set
rf_preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test)$predictions

# ensure they’re a factor with the same levels as the test outcome
rf_preds <- factor(
  rf_preds,
  levels = levels(test$Diabetes_012)
)


# Compute and print the confusion matrix
rf_cm <- confusionMatrix(rf_preds, test$Diabetes_012)

# Print 
cat("Random Forest:\n\n")
print(rf_cm)

```


```{r xgb, cache=TRUE}
#Train the Xgboost model

# Ensure test labels have the same levels as training
test$Diabetes_012 <- factor(test$Diabetes_012,
                            levels = levels(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012))

# Build matrices and DMatrices from your existing split
x_cols  <- setdiff(names(balanced_training_set), "Diabetes_012")
train_x <- as.matrix(balanced_training_set[, x_cols])
train_y <- as.numeric(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012) - 1L  # 0,1,2

test_x  <- as.matrix(test[, x_cols])
test_yf <- test$Diabetes_012

dtrain  <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_x, label = train_y)
dtest   <- xgb.DMatrix(data = test_x,  label = as.numeric(test_yf) - 1L)

# Train XGBoost with 500 boosting rounds
set.seed(123)
xgb_model <- xgboost(
  data        = dtrain,
  objective   = "multi:softprob",
  num_class   = 3,
  eval_metric = "mlogloss",
  nrounds     = 500,   
  verbose     = 0
)

# Predict & evaluate
pred_prob <- predict(xgb_model, dtest)
pred_mat  <- matrix(pred_prob, ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE)
pred_lab  <- factor(max.col(pred_mat) - 1L, levels = 0:2,
                    labels = levels(test_yf))

xgb_cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(pred_lab, test_yf)

# Print 
cat("XGBoost:\n\n")
print(xgb_cm)

```


8. Deep Learning Models
```{r tabnet-training, cache=TRUE}

# Full TabNet training & evaluation on balanced data with class‐weights

set.seed(123)

# Prepare training frame (ensure factor target with labels No/Pre/Yes)
df_full <- balanced_training_set
df_full$Diabetes_012 <- factor(
  as.integer(df_full$Diabetes_012) - 1L,
  levels = 0:2,
  labels = c("No","Pre","Yes")
)

# Class weights -> expand to per-sample weights
freqs         <- table(df_full$Diabetes_012)
inv_freqs     <- sum(freqs) / (length(freqs) * freqs)      # inverse frequency
class_weights <- as.numeric(inv_freqs)                      # length = 3 (No, Pre, Yes)
row_weights   <- class_weights[as.integer(df_full$Diabetes_012)]  # per-sample

# Config: single place for valid split + early stopping
full_config <- tabnet_config(
  num_steps               = 3,
  skip_importance         = TRUE,
  learn_rate              = 0.02,
  virtual_batch_size      = 4096,
  num_independent         = 2,
  num_shared              = 2,
  valid_split             = 0.20,
  early_stopping_monitor  = "valid_loss",
  early_stopping_patience = 3
)

# TabNet with class weights 
predictor_cols <- setdiff(names(df_full), "Diabetes_012")

set.seed(123)
tabnet_model <- tabnet_fit(
  x           = df_full[, predictor_cols],
  y           = df_full$Diabetes_012,
  config      = full_config,
  epochs      = 20,
  batch_size  = 256,
  num_workers = 0,
  weights     = row_weights
)

# Prepare hold-out test set (match factor levels)
true_factor <- factor(
  as.integer(test$Diabetes_012) - 1L,
  levels = 0:2,
  labels = c("No","Pre","Yes")
)
test_x <- test[, predictor_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Predict class labels on test set
preds     <- predict(tabnet_model, test_x, type = "class")
pred_class <- preds$.pred_class

# Accuracy & confusion matrix
acc <- mean(pred_class == true_factor)
cat(sprintf("TabNet test accuracy: %.2f%%\n\n", 100 * acc))

conf_mat_full <- confusionMatrix(pred_class, true_factor)
print(conf_mat_full)

```


```{r lstm-training, cache=TRUE}

#Train the LSTM model

# --- Prep ---
predictors <- setdiff(names(balanced_training_set), "Diabetes_012")

X_train <- as.matrix(balanced_training_set[, predictors, drop = FALSE])
X_test  <- as.matrix(test[, predictors, drop = FALSE])

mu <- colMeans(X_train)
sd <- apply(X_train, 2, sd); sd[sd == 0] <- 1
X_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu, scale = sd)
X_test  <- scale(X_test,  center = mu, scale = sd)

X_train_seq <- array(X_train, dim = c(nrow(X_train), 1L, ncol(X_train)))
X_test_seq  <- array(X_test,  dim = c(nrow(X_test),  1L, ncol(X_test)))

train_yf <- factor(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012, levels = c("No","Pre","Yes"))
test_yf  <- factor(test$Diabetes_012,                     levels = c("No","Pre","Yes"))

num_classes <- 3
y_train <- keras::to_categorical(as.integer(train_yf) - 1L, num_classes = num_classes)
y_test  <- keras::to_categorical(as.integer(test_yf)  - 1L, num_classes = num_classes)

# Simple LSTM model
lstm_model <- keras::keras_model_sequential() |>
  keras::layer_lstm(32, input_shape = c(1L, dim(X_train_seq)[3])) |>
  keras::layer_dropout(0.2) |>
  keras::layer_dense(num_classes, activation = "softmax")

lstm_model |>
  keras::compile(
    loss      = "categorical_crossentropy",
    optimizer = keras::optimizer_adam(learning_rate = 1e-3),
    metrics   = "accuracy"
  )

# Early stop (keeps best weights)
es_cb <- keras::callback_early_stopping(
  monitor              = "val_loss",
  patience             = 3,
  restore_best_weights = TRUE
)

# --- Train (uses 20% of train as validation) ---
history <- lstm_model |>
  keras::fit(
    X_train_seq, y_train,
    epochs           = 15,
    batch_size       = 128,
    validation_split = 0.2,
    callbacks        = list(es_cb),
    verbose          = 2
  )

# --- Evaluate on test set ---
res <- keras::evaluate(lstm_model, X_test_seq, y_test, verbose = 0)
cat(sprintf("LSTM test accuracy: %.3f\n", as.numeric(res["accuracy"])))

# --- Predict on test set ---
pred_probs <- predict(lstm_model, X_test_seq, verbose = 0)


# Convert probabilities to class labels
pred_labels <- factor(
  apply(pred_probs, 1, which.max) - 1L,
  levels = 0:2,
  labels = levels(train_yf)
)

# Confusion matrix
lstm_cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(pred_labels, test_yf)
print(lstm_cm)

```


9. Evaluation
```{r cache=TRUE}

# Compute Accuracy, Balanced Accuracy, and Macro-F1 from confusion matrices
metrics_from_cm <- function(cm) {
  byc <- as.data.frame(cm$byClass)
  bal_acc <- mean(byc[,"Balanced Accuracy"], na.rm = TRUE)
  macro_f1 <- mean(byc[,"F1"], na.rm = TRUE)
  c(
    Accuracy          = unname(cm$overall["Accuracy"]),
    Balanced_Accuracy = bal_acc,
    Macro_F1          = macro_f1
  )
}

# Build comparison tables of model performance
results_initial <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Random Forest", "XGBoost", "Logistic Regression", "TabNet", "LSTM"),
  rbind(
    metrics_from_cm(rf_cm),           # RF CM
    metrics_from_cm(xgb_cm),          # XGB CM
    metrics_from_cm(lr_cm),           # LR CM
    metrics_from_cm(conf_mat_full),   # TabNet CM
    metrics_from_cm(lstm_cm)          # LSTM CM
  )
)

# Format as percentages with 1 decimal place
results_initial_fmt <- results_initial %>%
  mutate(
    Accuracy          = sprintf("%.1f%%", Accuracy * 100),
    Balanced_Accuracy = sprintf("%.1f%%", Balanced_Accuracy * 100),
    Macro_F1          = sprintf("%.1f%%", Macro_F1 * 100)
  )

knitr::kable(
  results_initial_fmt,
  caption = "Model comparison — Accuracy, Balanced Accuracy, Macro-F1."
)

```


```{r}
# --- Grouped bar chart for the models ---

results_long <- results_initial %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Accuracy, Balanced_Accuracy, Macro_F1),
    names_to = "Metric",
    values_to = "Score"
  )

# Order models by Accuracy for clearer comparisons
order_by_acc <- results_initial %>% arrange(desc(Accuracy)) %>% pull(Model)
results_long$Model <- factor(results_long$Model, levels = order_by_acc)

ggplot(results_long, aes(x = Model, y = Score, fill = Metric)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Score * 100)),   # percentage labels
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            vjust = -0.25, size = 3.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Performance Comparison",
    y = "Score (%)",
    x = "Model"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0,1)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

```


10. Hyperparameter Tuning
```{r cache=TRUE}
#Hyperparameter tuning for random forest with 500 trees


# Take a sample of 5000 rows for tuning only
set.seed(123)
sample_idx <- sample(nrow(balanced_training_set), 5000)
tune_sample <- balanced_training_set[sample_idx, ]

# Split predictors/outcome
train_x <- tune_sample %>% select(-Diabetes_012)
train_y <- tune_sample$Diabetes_012

# CV setup
rf_ctrl <- trainControl(
  method     = "cv",
  number     = 3,     # lighter than 5- or 10-fold
  classProbs = TRUE
)

# Grid
rf_grid <- expand.grid(
  mtry          = 5:10,
  splitrule     = "extratrees",
  min.node.size = 1
)

# Hyperparameter tuning for Random Fores
set.seed(123)
rf_tuned_subset <- train(
  x         = train_x,
  y         = train_y,
  method    = "ranger",
  tuneGrid  = rf_grid,
  trControl = rf_ctrl,
  num.trees = 200,     # lighter for tuning step
  metric    = "Accuracy"
)

# 5) Results
print(rf_tuned_subset)

```



```{r cache=TRUE}
# === Rebuild the RF model with the best hyperparameters ===
set.seed(123)

# Ensure test outcome levels match training
test$Diabetes_012 <- factor(test$Diabetes_012,
                            levels = levels(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012))

# Tuned params from RF tuning
rf_best <- as.list(rf_tuned_subset$bestTune)   # mtry, splitrule, min.node.size
str(rf_best)


# Final refit on FULL balanced training set
set.seed(123)
rf_final <- ranger::ranger(
  Diabetes_012 ~ .,
  data            = balanced_training_set,
  num.trees       = 500,
  mtry            = rf_best$mtry,
  splitrule       = rf_best$splitrule,
  min.node.size   = rf_best$min.node.size,
  sample.fraction = 0.632,
  probability     = TRUE,
  importance      = "none",
  num.threads     = 1,
  verbose         = TRUE
)


# Evaluate on test set (use only predictors)
feat_cols   <- setdiff(names(test), "Diabetes_012")
rf_pred_obj <- predict(rf_final, data = test[, feat_cols, drop = FALSE])

rf_prob <- as.data.frame(rf_pred_obj$predictions)
rf_prob <- rf_prob[, levels(test$Diabetes_012), drop = FALSE]

rf_pred <- factor(
  colnames(rf_prob)[max.col(as.matrix(rf_prob), ties.method = "first")],
  levels = levels(test$Diabetes_012)
)

rf_tuned_cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test$Diabetes_012)

# Print 
cat("Random Forest (Tuned):\n\n")
print(rf_tuned_cm)

```


```{r cache=TRUE}
# Hyperparameter tuning for xgboost model

# --- Tuning subset ---
set.seed(123)
tune_idx <- caret::createDataPartition(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012, p = 0.15, list = FALSE)
tune_x   <- balanced_training_set[tune_idx, ] |> dplyr::select(-Diabetes_012)
tune_y   <- balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012[tune_idx]

# --- 3-fold CV, random search (~30 configs) ---
xgb_ctrl <- caret::trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 3,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = multiClassSummary,
  allowParallel   = TRUE,
  search          = "random",
  verboseIter     = FALSE
)

# Hyperparameter tuning for XGBoost 
set.seed(123)
xgb_tuned <- caret::train(
  x              = tune_x,
  y              = tune_y,
  method         = "xgbTree",
  trControl      = xgb_ctrl,
  tuneLength     = 30,     # ~30 random configs
  metric         = "Accuracy",
  verbose        = FALSE,
  nthread        = 2
)

# Inspect & save best params for refitting the model
print(xgb_tuned$bestTune)


```


```{r}
# === XGBoost: Final refit on FULL training set + evaluation ===
set.seed(123)

# Ensure test outcome factor levels match training
test$Diabetes_012 <- factor(test$Diabetes_012, 
                            levels = levels(balanced_training_set$Diabetes_012))

# Take best params directly from tuning
best <- xgb_tuned$bestTune

# Final refit (same params, trained on full balanced set)
xgb_final <- caret::train(
  Diabetes_012 ~ .,
  data         = balanced_training_set,
  method       = "xgbTree",
  trControl    = caret::trainControl(method = "none", classProbs = TRUE),
  tuneGrid     = best,
  metric       = "Accuracy",
  verbose      = FALSE,
  nthread      = 2
)

# Evaluate on test set
xgb_final_pred <- predict(xgb_final, newdata = test)
xgb_final_cm   <- caret::confusionMatrix(xgb_final_pred, test$Diabetes_012)

cat("XGBoost (Tuned):\n\n")
print(xgb_final_cm)

# Probabilities for hybrid/stacking
xgb_prob <- predict(xgb_final, newdata = test, type = "prob")
xgb_prob <- xgb_prob[, levels(test$Diabetes_012), drop = FALSE]

best <- xgb_tuned$bestTune
best$nrounds <- best$nrounds 

```


```{r}
# === Hybrid RF(tuned) + Xgboost(tuned) ===
set.seed(123)

want_levels  <- c("No","Pre","Yes")
feature_cols <- setdiff(names(balanced_training_set), "Diabetes_012")

# Probabilities
rf_prob  <- predict(rf_final, data = test[, feature_cols])$predictions
rf_prob  <- as.data.frame(rf_prob)[, want_levels, drop = FALSE]
xgb_prob <- predict(xgb_final, newdata = test[, feature_cols], type = "prob")
xgb_prob <- xgb_prob[, want_levels, drop = FALSE]

# Combine RF + XGBoost predictions (hybrid ensemble)
hybrid_prob <- rf_prob

# Only override to XGB's "Yes" when XGB is clearly more confident
delta <- 0.10   # margin (tune on validation if desired)
thr   <- 0.60   # absolute confidence threshold

use_xgb_yes <- (xgb_prob$Yes - rf_prob$Yes >= delta) & (xgb_prob$Yes >= thr)
hybrid_prob$Yes[use_xgb_yes] <- xgb_prob$Yes[use_xgb_yes]

# keep sums ≤ 1 by re-normalising rows (safe and simple)
hybrid_prob <- hybrid_prob / pmax(rowSums(hybrid_prob), 1e-12)

# Labels and evaluation
hybrid_pred <- factor(colnames(hybrid_prob)[max.col(as.matrix(hybrid_prob), "first")],
                      levels = want_levels)
hybrid_cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(hybrid_pred, test$Diabetes_012)

cat("Hybrid Model (RF + XGBoost):\n\n")
print(hybrid_cm)
```


```{r cache=TRUE}

# Compute Accuracy, Balanced Accuracy, and Macro-F1 from confusion matrices

# Helper to extract metrics
metrics_from_cm <- function(cm) {
  byc <- as.data.frame(cm$byClass)
  bal_acc <- mean(byc[, "Balanced Accuracy"], na.rm = TRUE)
  macro_f1 <- mean(byc[, "F1"], na.rm = TRUE)
  c(
    Accuracy         = unname(cm$overall["Accuracy"]),
    BalancedAccuracy = bal_acc,
    MacroF1          = macro_f1
  )
}

# Build results table (replace objects with your CM names)
results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Logistic Regression", "Random Forest (tuned)", "XGBoost (tuned)",
            "TabNet", "LSTM", "Hybrid (RF+XGB)"),
  rbind(
    metrics_from_cm(lr_cm),
    metrics_from_cm(rf_tuned_cm),
    metrics_from_cm(xgb_final_cm),
    metrics_from_cm(conf_mat_full),
    metrics_from_cm(lstm_cm),
    metrics_from_cm(hybrid_cm)
  )
)

# Table (sorted by Accuracy, formatted as %)
results_sorted <- results %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(Accuracy))

results_print <- results_sorted %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Accuracy         = sprintf("%.1f%%", Accuracy * 100),
    BalancedAccuracy = sprintf("%.1f%%", BalancedAccuracy * 100),
    MacroF1          = sprintf("%.1f%%", MacroF1 * 100)
  )

knitr::kable(
  results_print,
  caption = "Model comparison — Accuracy, Balanced Accuracy, Macro-F1 (sorted by Accuracy)."
)


```


```{r cache=TRUE}

# --- Grouped bar chart for tuned + hybrid models ---

results_long <- results_sorted %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Accuracy, BalancedAccuracy, MacroF1),
    names_to = "Metric",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = results_sorted$Model))

ggplot(results_long, aes(x = Model, y = Score, fill = Metric)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Score * 100)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            vjust = -0.25, size = 3.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Performance Comparison (sorted by Accuracy)",
    y = "Score (%)",
    x = "Model"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

```


13. Save the best model (RF tuned)
```{r}
# Save tuned Random Forest model + evaluation objects
saveRDS(list(best_tune = rf_best, num.trees = 500, sample.fraction = 0.632),
        "rf_final_meta.rds")
saveRDS(rf_final,    "rf_final.rds")
saveRDS(rf_tuned_cm, "rf_final_confmat.rds")
saveRDS(rf_prob,     "rf_final_test_prob.rds")

```
